claim: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is analogous to USB-C for AI applications - a universal standard for connecting tools and context to AI apps across different vendors
actionable: Design your AI tool integrations using MCP standards to enable portability across Claude, VS Code, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible applications
source_title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Explained in 20 Minutes

claim: MCP servers expose three key primitives: prompt templates (static boilerplate with input swapping), resources (static data via GET-like requests), and tools (arbitrary functions)
actionable: Structure your MCP server by categorizing agent-facing capabilities as prompts for templates, resources for data lookups, and tools for side effects/computations
source_title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Explained in 20 Minutes

claim: MCP clients are responsible for discovering server capabilities, receiving data, and managing tool execution since LLMs are text generators and cannot execute tools themselves
actionable: When building MCP applications, ensure your client implementation handles capability discovery, data marshaling, and safe tool execution with appropriate error handling
source_title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Explained in 20 Minutes

claim: LLMs do not need 10,000 tokens of system prompt explaining they are coding agents because models are post-trained on coding agent behavior and already understand the role
actionable: Reduce system prompt verbosity for well-trained models by assuming foundational knowledge from post-training and focusing on your specific context and requirements
source_title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Explained in 20 Minutes

claim: Models need explicit specification of which prompts and resources to use but automatically access available tools - requiring different interaction patterns for different primitive types
actionable: When designing agent prompts, explicitly list required resources and prompt templates but assume tools will be discovered and called automatically when needed
source_title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Explained in 20 Minutes
